pr_summary: This PR adds new functionality to the codebase.
verdict_reason: The review found critical-severity issues requiring attention.

findings.title: Infinite Loop with No Timeout or Retry Limit
findings.description: The while loop in the pollOrderStatus function will run indefinitely if the order status never changes, holding the thread/connection/memory indefinitely.
findings.suggestion: Add a maximum iteration count, a timeout, or a circuit breaker to prevent the loop from running indefinitely.

findings.line_content: export async function cancelOrder(orderId: string, userId: string): Promise<void> {
findings.title: Missing test: cancelOrder called with null or undefined orderId
findings.description: This test is important because it would catch a potential crash when trying to cancel an order with a null or undefined orderId. The function does not check for null or undefined orderId before trying to find the order in the database.
findings.suggestion: Test cancelOrder with null and undefined orderId, and verify that it throws an error or handles it correctly.

findings.title: NULL DEREFERENCE
findings.description: If the orderRepo.findById(orderId) returns null or undefined, accessing order.userEmail will throw a TypeError or NullPointerException, causing a production crash.
findings.suggestion: Add a null check before accessing order properties: if (order) { ... } else { throw new Error(`Order not found: ${orderId}`); }

findings.line_content: export async function bulkCancelOrders(orderIds: string[]): Promise<void> {
findings.title: Missing test: bulkCancelOrders called with null or undefined orderIds
findings.description: This test is important because it would catch a potential crash when trying to cancel orders with a null or undefined orderIds. The function does not check for null or undefined orderIds before trying to cancel the orders.
findings.suggestion: Test bulkCancelOrders with null and undefined orderIds, and verify that it throws an error or handles it correctly.

findings.title: SILENT UNDEFINED / NaN PROPAGATION
findings.description: If the discountCode is not found in the discounts object, the function will return NaN, which can corrupt downstream calculations without throwing an error.
findings.suggestion: Add a check for undefined discountCode: if (!(discountCode in discounts)) { throw new Error(`Invalid discountCode: ${discountCode}`); }

positive_observations: The code follows a consistent structure.
Function naming is clear and descriptive.
missing_tests: This test is important because it would catch a potential crash when trying to cancel an order with a null or undefined orderId. The function does not check for null or undefined orderId before trying to find the order in the database.

This test is important because it would catch a potential crash when trying to cancel orders with a null or undefined orderIds. The function does not check for null or undefined orderIds before trying to cancel the orders.
